title: The Group Behind ‘2000 Mules’ Is Back With Another Election Conspiracy Film
gist: True the Vote, creators of the debunked 2020 election-fraud film 2000 Mules, is producing a new documentary called 'Trap' with Detroit pastor Lorenzo Sewell that recycles a court-dismissed 2024 lawsuit's unsubstantiated claims of systemic ballot fraud targeting Black voters in Detroit and other majority-Black cities ahead of the 2026 midterms.

themes.theme: New film revives a legally dismissed claim

insights.claim: True the Vote's new documentary 'Trap' is built around claims first made in a 2024 lawsuit by Detroit activist Ramon Jackson alleging Democratic officials Jocelyn Benson and Janice Winfrey orchestrated fraudulent voter registrations dating to 2017, a case that was dismissed for lack of standing and insufficient evidence.
insights.speaker: David Gilbert
insights.so_what: Signals a specific, previously court-rejected claim is being repackaged as documentary evidence ahead of the midterms, relevant to assessing election-disinformation risk into the 2026 cycle.
insights.locator: The film is set to repeat many of the claims

insights.claim: Pastor Lorenzo Sewell, central to the documentary, admits he has no evidence for his claim that Democrats are voting on behalf of poor Black people without their knowledge, and describes his detection method as flagging voter names 'not conducive to the names of our community.'
insights.speaker: David Gilbert
insights.so_what: Exposes the specific, non-rigorous methodology likely to underpin viral pre-midterm election-fraud claims, useful for pattern recognition when similar claims recirculate online.
insights.quote: There is a pattern right now happening in our country where Democrats are voting for poor black people without their knowledge... Black people don't vote absentee.
insights.locator: 'There is a pattern right now happening in our country'

insights.claim: A study published the same week directly contradicts Sewell's central claim, finding that voting by mail is actually more common among Black voters in communities with high hate-crime rates, not less common as Sewell asserts.
insights.speaker: David Gilbert
insights.so_what: Gives a concrete, citable rebuttal data point to counter the documentary's central claim once it begins circulating publicly.
insights.locator: However, a study published this week

insights.claim: True the Vote's 2022 film 2000 Mules, which alleged ballot-trafficking based on the group's own data, was pulled from distribution in 2024 after producer Salem Media Group settled a defamation lawsuit with a Georgia man wrongly identified in the film as a 'mule.'
insights.speaker: David Gilbert
insights.so_what: Establishes a documented base rate of factual and legal failure for this production team's prior election-fraud film, informing how much credibility to assign to the new one.
insights.locator: 2000 Mules, which was released by right-wing activist Dinesh d'Souza

insights.claim: True the Vote cofounder Gregg Phillips has teased the new documentary on Truth Social alongside Tina Peters (a pardoned former county clerk tied to election-system tampering) and linked it to a DOJ warning letter on noncitizen voting, indicating a coordinated pre-midterm messaging push around the film's release.
insights.speaker: David Gilbert
insights.so_what: Signals the documentary is timed and networked to amplify broader 2026 midterm election-fraud narratives, relevant for platforms and advertisers assessing election-disinformation exposure this cycle.
insights.quote: Just wait until you learn about the documentary Catherine and Pastor Lorenzo are launching... Makes mules look like childs [sic] play.
insights.locator: Phillips also posted a picture of himself

insights.claim: David Becker of the Center for Election Innovation and Research, a former DOJ Voting Section attorney, argues that because the 2020 election is 'the most scrutinized election in world history,' new fraud claims surfacing six years later are 'clearly false' and designed to sow doubt about election outcomes the filmmakers dislike.
insights.speaker: David Gilbert
entities: Center for Election Innovation and Research
insights.so_what: Frames the expert-consensus baseline for evaluating the documentary's credibility once it is released and begins circulating ahead of the midterms.
insights.quote: The 2020 election is the most scrutinized election in world history, so any claims [True the Vote] makes six years later are clearly false and more designed to cause people to doubt the outcome of elections that the filmmakers might not like the outcome of.
